name: Woodford v. Visciotti
question: Was a state court’s application of the Supreme Court’s ineffective assistance of counsel precedent as requiring the defendant to show prejudice by a preponderance of the evidence objectively unreasonable?
description: A case in which the Court determined that a federal court cannot reverse a state court’s decision on habeas review when the state court reasonably applied precedent requiring the defendant show prejudice by strong evidence of ineffective counsel.
